title: papaver dubium proles collinum
intro: Papaver dubium proles collinum, also known as the Blue Poppy or Meconopsis, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid compounds, including meconic acid and meconidine. The health benefits of Papaver dubium proles collinum include pain relief, reduced inflammation, and relaxation of muscles, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as arthritis, muscle spasms, and anxiety. The plant is typically harvested for its roots and leaves, which are then used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions.
